title: Engineering Digest Candidates Inventory (v3.2)
description: Output artefact of Phase 1 (Beat sweep / research). Every top-tier beat must appear, even if its candidates array is empty.

state_snapshot.description: Selected fields from engineering-digest-state.json that drove the research, captured for auditability.

beats.description: One entry per operational concern (v3.4). Mandatory concerns: management_teams, ways_of_working, operating_at_scale, banking_fintech_loyalty. AI is the substrate that runs through all four — not a fifth concern. Classify candidates by the operational decision they trigger, not by surface topic.

queries_run.description: Verbatim list of search queries used. Minimum 3, target 3-5.

null_finding.description: Required when candidates is empty. Must reference what was searched and why nothing qualified. 'Nothing came up' is not acceptable.

secondary.description: Candidate pools for slots that don't map to top-tier beats.

p0_active.description: Active Priority 0 stories carried from prior issues with new developments this week.

headline.description: The story in a single line. Not the source's headline verbatim — a paraphrase that captures the so-what.

beat.description: Optional in candidate-level (the beat is set by the parent in the beats array). Used in the secondary pools.

summary.description: 2–3 sentences. Enough that the curation phase can rank without re-fetching the source.

so_what.description: One sentence on why an engineering leader should care. Must answer 'what would change in their week if they read this?'

tier_guess.description: Researcher's first-pass slot estimate. Curation phase makes the final call.

uk_me_relevance.description: How directly the story touches a UK or Middle East banking/fintech engineering team's week.

narrative_tension.description: Does the story have an arc, controversy, surprise, or stake? Routine version releases default to low.

urgency_classification.description: v3.2 audience-fit gate. act_this_week = reader needs to do something or have a conversation in the next 7 days. decide_this_month = reader has a decision to make in the next 30 days. general_awareness = useful context, no near-term action. Only act_this_week and high-impact decide_this_month stories are eligible for anchor slots (lead, QT1, QT2). Version EOLs, minor version bumps, and most platform updates default to general_awareness unless impact is widespread right now (real-time customer outage, active CVE in mass exploitation, regulatory deadline this week for the reader's own organisation — not their customers').

secondary_sources.description: Optional. Confirmation or context links beyond the primary source.

notes.description: Optional. Anything the curator should know — e.g. 'related to Issue 8 lead', 'overlaps with the security beat candidate X', 'piece is paywalled'.

jargon_terms.description: Optional. Capitalised technical terms or proper nouns in this candidate that will need glossing or a Jargon Watch sidebar (per v3.7 rule — broadened to include lowercase / hyphenated specialist terms and any term appearing 3+ times in one section).

chartable_data.description: Optional (v1.30). Free-text note flagging whether this candidate has data, structure, or content that would chart, table, or diagram well. Examples: 'three comparable adoption metrics across vendors', 'source describes a 4-step process the team rolled out', 'before/after metrics from a single-team experiment', 'architecture: 5 components with dependency arrows'. Curation and planning phases use this to allocate visual primitives (inline SVG chart, compare-table, process-flow, by-the-numbers callout) per the v1.30 visual density rules.
